devdoc.details: Utility library for validating signatures from both EOAs and smart contract wallets. Supports EIP-6492 for counterfactual (not-yet-deployed) smart contract wallets.

validateSignature(bytes32,bytes32,bytes,.details: Validates a signature for both EOAs and smart contract wallets. Tries EIP-6492 first (for counterfactual contracts),  then ECDSA (for EOAs),  then ERC-1271 (for deployed smart contracts). All paths try standard digest first, then minified digest (for Ledger). For some cases, e.g. Ledger, signing does not work because the payload is too large. For these cases, the user can sign a minified structHash instead. The user should be shown the source data that was used to construct the minified structHash so that they can verify it independently. Note: This function is not `view` because EIP-6492 validation may deploy the counterfactual contract via a factory call.

params.signatureType: The signature type string for error messages

devdoc.title: SignatureUtils
